info.title: LibreFang API
info.description: LibreFang Agent Operating System — REST API for managing AI agents, tools, workflows, and more.

get.summary: GET /a2a/agents — List all A2A agent cards.

post.summary: POST /a2a/tasks/send — Submit a task to an agent via A2A.

200.description: Submit a task to an agent via A2A

get.summary: GET /a2a/tasks/{id} — Get task status from the task store.

post.summary: POST /a2a/tasks/{id}/cancel — Cancel a tracked task.

get.summary: GET /api/a2a/agents — List discovered external A2A agents.
GET /api/a2a/agents/{id} — Get a specific external A2A agent by index, URL, or name.

200.description: Get a specific external A2A agent

post.summary: POST /api/a2a/discover — Discover a new external A2A agent by URL.

200.description: Discover an external A2A agent by URL

post.summary: POST /api/a2a/send — Send a task to an external A2A agent.

200.description: Send a task to an external A2A agent

get.summary: GET /api/a2a/tasks/{id}/status — Get task status from an external A2A agent.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL of the external A2A agent

get.summary: GET /api/agents — List agents with optional filtering, pagination, and sorting.
get.description:
Query parameters (all optional — omitting them returns all agents):
  - `q`: free-text search across name and description (case-insensitive)
  - `status`: filter by lifecycle state (e.g. "running", "suspended")
  - `limit` / `offset`: pagination
  - `sort`: field to sort by — "name", "created_at", "last_active", "state"
  - `order`: "asc" (default) or "desc"

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field: name, created_at, last_active, state

post.summary: POST /api/agents — Spawn a new agent.
POST /api/agents/bulk — Create multiple agents at once.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/agents/bulk — Delete multiple agents at once.

requestBody.description: Array of agent IDs to delete

post.summary: POST /api/agents/bulk/start — Set multiple agents to Full mode.

requestBody.description: Array of agent IDs to start

200.description: Start multiple agents (set to Full mode)

post.summary: POST /api/agents/bulk/stop — Stop multiple agents' current runs.

requestBody.description: Array of agent IDs to stop

get.summary: GET /api/agents/:id — Get a single agent's detailed info.

200.description: Partially update an agent (name, description, model, system prompt)

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/clone — Clone an agent with its workspace files.

requestBody.description: New name for the cloned agent

200.description: Clone an agent with its workspace files

patch.summary: PATCH /api/agents/{id}/config — Hot-update agent name, description, system prompt, and identity.

200.description: Hot-update agent name, description, system prompt, identity, and model

get.summary: GET /api/agents/:id/deliveries — List recent delivery receipts for an agent.

200.description: List recent delivery receipts for an agent

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/files — List workspace identity files.

200.description: List workspace identity files for an agent

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/files/{filename} — Read a workspace identity file.

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/files/{filename} — Write a workspace identity file.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/agents/{id}/files/{filename} — Delete a workspace identity file.
DELETE /api/agents/{id}/history — Clear ALL conversation history for an agent.

200.description: Clear all conversation history for an agent

patch.summary: PATCH /api/agents/{id}/identity — Update an agent's visual identity.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/mcp_servers — Get an agent's MCP server assignment info.

200.description: Get an agent's MCP server assignment info

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/mcp_servers — Update an agent's MCP server allowlist.

200.description: Update an agent's MCP server allowlist

get.summary: GET /api/agents/:id/memory/export — Export all KV memory for an agent as JSON.

post.summary: POST /api/agents/:id/memory/import — Import KV memory from JSON into an agent.
post.description:
Accepts a JSON body with a `kv` object mapping string keys to JSON values.
Optionally accepts `clear_existing: true` to wipe existing memory before import.

post.summary: POST /api/agents/:id/message — Send a message to an agent.
POST /api/agents/:id/message/stream — SSE streaming response.

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/:id/mode — Change an agent's operational mode.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/:id/session — Get agent session (conversation history).

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/session/compact — Trigger LLM session compaction.
POST /api/agents/{id}/session/reboot — Hard-reboot an agent's session (full clear, no summary).

200.description: Hard-reboot an agent's session without saving summary

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/session/reset — Reset an agent's session.

200.description: List all sessions for an agent

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/sessions — Create a new session for an agent.

requestBody.description: Optional label for the new session

200.description: Create a new session for an agent

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/by-label/:label — Find session by label (scoped to agent).

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/sessions/import — Import a previously exported session.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/sessions/{session_id}/export — Export a session for hibernation.

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/sessions/{session_id}/switch — Switch to an existing session.

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/skills — Get an agent's skill assignment info.

200.description: Get an agent's skill assignment info

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/skills — Update an agent's skill allowlist.

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/stop — Cancel an agent's current LLM run.

200.description: Cancel an agent's current LLM run

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/tools — Get an agent's tool allowlist/blocklist.

200.description: Get an agent's tool allowlist and blocklist

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/{id}/tools — Update an agent's tool allowlist/blocklist.

200.description: Update an agent's tool allowlist and blocklist

get.summary: GET /api/agents/{id}/traces — Get decision traces from the agent's most recent message.
get.description:
Returns structured traces showing why each tool was selected during the last
agent loop execution. Useful for debugging, auditing, and optimization.

200.description: Get decision traces from the agent's most recent message

put.summary: PUT /api/agents/:id — Update an agent (currently: re-set manifest fields).

post.summary: POST /api/agents/{id}/upload — Upload a file attachment.
post.description:
Accepts raw body bytes. The client must set:
- `Content-Type` header (e.g., `image/png`, `text/plain`, `application/pdf`)
- `X-Filename` header (original filename)

200.description: Upload a file attachment for an agent

get.summary: GET /api/approvals — List pending and recent approval requests.
get.description:
Transforms field names to match the dashboard template expectations:
`action_summary` → `action`, `agent_id` → `agent_name`, `requested_at` → `created_at`.

get.summary: GET /api/approvals/{id} — Get a single approval request by ID.

post.summary: POST /api/approvals/{id}/reject — Reject a pending request.

get.summary: GET /api/audit/recent — Get recent audit log entries.
GET /api/audit/verify — Verify the audit chain integrity.
GET /api/auth/callback — Handle the OAuth2 authorization code callback (browser redirect).

post.summary: POST /api/auth/callback — Handle the OAuth2 callback (programmatic clients).
POST /api/auth/introspect — Validate a token and return its claims.
post.description: Follows RFC 7662 conventions: returns `{"active": true/false, ...}`.

get.summary: GET /api/auth/login — Redirect to the external identity provider (legacy single-provider).
GET /api/auth/login/:provider — Redirect to a specific provider.
GET /api/auth/providers — List available authentication providers.
GET /api/auth/userinfo — Return info about the currently authenticated user.
get.description:
If a valid JWT is in the Authorization header and JWKS validation succeeds,
returns the decoded claims. Otherwise falls back to provider userinfo endpoint.

post.summary: POST /api/backup — Create a backup archive of kernel state.
post.description:
Returns the backup metadata including the filename. The archive is stored
in `<home_dir>/backups/` with a timestamped filename.

get.summary: GET /api/backups — List existing backups.
GET /api/bindings — List all agent bindings.

post.summary: POST /api/bindings — Add a new agent binding.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/bindings/:index — Remove a binding by index.

get.summary: GET /api/budget — Current budget status (limits, spend, % used).

put.summary: PUT /api/budget — Update global budget limits (in-memory only, not persisted to config.toml).

get.summary: GET /api/budget/agents — Per-agent cost ranking (top spenders).
GET /api/budget/agents/{id} — Per-agent budget/quota status.

put.summary: PUT /api/budget/agents/{id} — Update per-agent budget limits at runtime.

get.summary: GET /api/catalog/status — Check last catalog sync time.

post.summary: POST /api/catalog/update — Sync model catalog from the remote repository.
post.description:
Downloads the latest catalog TOML files from GitHub and caches them locally.
After syncing, the kernel's in-memory catalog is refreshed.

get.summary: GET /api/channels — List all 40 channel adapters with status and field metadata.

post.summary: POST /api/channels/reload — Manually trigger a channel hot-reload from disk config.
POST /api/channels/wechat/qr/start — Request a QR code from iLink for WeChat login.

get.summary: GET /api/channels/wechat/qr/status — Poll iLink for QR scan confirmation.

post.summary: POST /api/channels/whatsapp/qr/start — Start a WhatsApp Web QR login session.
post.description:
If a WhatsApp Web gateway is available (e.g. a Baileys-based bridge process),
this proxies the request and returns a base64 QR code data URL. If no gateway
is running, it returns instructions to set one up.

get.summary: GET /api/channels/whatsapp/qr/status — Poll for QR scan completion.
get.description:
After calling `/qr/start`, the frontend polls this to check if the user
has scanned the QR code and the WhatsApp Web session is connected.

post.summary: POST /api/channels/{name}/configure — Save channel secrets + config fields.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/channels/{name}/configure — Remove channel secrets + config section.

post.summary: POST /api/channels/{name}/test — Connectivity check + optional live test message.
post.description:
Accepts an optional JSON body with `channel_id` (for Discord/Slack) or `chat_id`
(for Telegram). When provided, sends a real test message to verify the bot can
post to that channel.

get.summary: GET /api/clawhub/browse — Browse ClawHub skills by sort order.
get.description:
Query parameters:
- `sort` — sort order: "trending", "downloads", "stars", "updated", "rating" (default: "trending")
- `limit` — max results (default: 20, max: 50)
- `cursor` — pagination cursor from previous response

200.description: Browse ClawHub skills by sort order

post.summary: POST /api/clawhub/install — Install a skill from ClawHub.
post.description:
Runs the full security pipeline: SHA256 verification, format detection,
manifest security scan, prompt injection scan, and binary dependency check.

get.summary: GET /api/clawhub/search — Search ClawHub skills using vector/semantic search.
get.description:
Query parameters:
- `q` — search query (required)
- `limit` — max results (default: 20, max: 50)

get.summary: GET /api/clawhub/skill/{slug} — Get detailed info about a ClawHub skill.

200.description: Get detailed info about a ClawHub skill

get.summary: GET /api/clawhub/skill/{slug}/code — Fetch the source code (SKILL.md) of a ClawHub skill.

200.description: Fetch source code of a ClawHub skill

get.summary: GET /api/commands — List available chat commands (for dynamic slash menu).
GET /api/commands/{name} — Lookup a single command by name.
GET /api/comms/events — Return recent inter-agent communication events.
get.description:
Sources from both the event bus (for lifecycle events with full context)
and the audit log (for message/spawn/kill events that are always captured).

get.summary: GET /api/comms/events/stream — SSE stream of inter-agent communication events.
get.description: Polls the audit log every 500ms for new inter-agent events.

post.summary: POST /api/comms/send — Send a message from one agent to another.
POST /api/comms/task — Post a task to the agent task queue.

200.description: Post a task to the agent task queue

get.summary: GET /api/comms/topology — Build agent topology graph from registry.
GET /api/config — Get kernel configuration (secrets redacted).

post.summary: POST /api/config/reload — Reload configuration from disk and apply hot-reloadable changes.
post.description:
Reads the config file, diffs against current config, validates the new config,
and applies hot-reloadable actions (approval policy, cron limits, etc.).
Returns the reload plan showing what changed and what was applied.

get.summary: GET /api/config/schema — Return a simplified JSON description of the config structure.

post.summary: POST /api/config/set — Set a single config value and persist to config.toml.
post.description:
Accepts JSON `{ "path": "section.key", "value": "..." }`.
Writes the value to the TOML config file and triggers a reload.

200.description: Set a single config value and persist

get.summary: GET /api/cron/jobs/{id}/status — Get status of a specific cron job.

post.summary:

GET /api/hands — List all hand definitions (marketplace).
GET /api/hands/active — List active hand instances.

200.description: Install a hand from TOML content

POST /api/hands/instances/{id}/resume — Resume a paused hand instance.

post.summary: POST /api/hands/reload — Reload hand definitions from disk.

get.summary: GET /api/hands/{hand_id} — Get a single hand definition with requirements check.

post.summary: POST /api/hands/{hand_id}/activate — Activate a hand (spawns agent).
POST /api/hands/{hand_id}/check-deps — Re-check dependency status for a hand.

200.description: Re-check dependency status for a hand

get.summary: GET /api/hands/{hand_id}/settings — Get settings schema and current values for a hand.

200.description: Get settings schema and current values

put.summary: PUT /api/hands/{hand_id}/settings — Update settings for a hand instance.

200.description: Update settings for a hand instance

get.summary:
GET /api/health — Minimal liveness probe (public, no auth required).
Returns only status and version to prevent information leakage.
Use GET /api/health/detail for full diagnostics (requires auth).
GET /api/health/detail — Full health diagnostics (requires auth).

post.summary: POST /hooks/agent — Run an isolated agent turn via webhook.
post.description:
Sends a message directly to the specified agent and returns the response.
This enables external systems (CI/CD, Slack, etc.) to trigger agent work.

post.summary: POST /hooks/wake — Inject a system event via webhook trigger.
post.description:
Publishes a custom event through the kernel's event system, which can
trigger proactive agents that subscribe to the event type.

post.summary: POST /api/init — Quick initialization (detect provider, write config, reload).
post.description: Skips if config.toml already exists. Returns the detected provider/model.

get.summary: GET /api/logs/stream — SSE endpoint for real-time audit log streaming.
get.description:
Streams new audit entries as Server-Sent Events. Accepts optional query
parameters for filtering:
  - `level`  — filter by classified level (info, warn, error)
  - `filter` — text substring filter across action/detail/agent_id
  - `token`  — auth token (for EventSource clients that cannot set headers)

A heartbeat ping is sent every 15 seconds to keep the connection alive.
The endpoint polls the audit log every second and sends only new entries
(tracked by sequence number). On first connect, existing entries are sent
as a backfill so the client has immediate context.

get.summary: GET /api/marketplace/search — Search the FangHub marketplace.
GET /api/mcp/catalog — List all installable MCP catalog entries.
GET /api/mcp/catalog/{id} — Single catalog entry detail.
GET /api/mcp/health — Health snapshot across all configured MCP servers.

200.description: Health snapshot for all configured MCP servers

post.summary: POST /api/mcp/reload — Re-read the catalog and reconnect MCP servers.

200.description: Reload catalog and reconnect MCP servers

get.summary: GET /api/mcp/servers — List configured MCP servers and their tools.

200.description: List configured MCP servers and their tools

post.summary: POST /api/mcp/servers — Add a new MCP server configuration.
post.description:
Expects a JSON body matching `McpServerConfigEntry` (name, transport, timeout_secs, env).
Persists to config.toml and triggers a config reload.

200.description: Add a new MCP server configuration

get.summary: GET /api/mcp/servers/{name} — Retrieve a single MCP server by name.
get.description:
Returns the configured server entry plus live connection status and tools
if the server is currently connected.

put.summary: PUT /api/mcp/servers/{name} — Update an existing MCP server configuration.
put.description:
Replaces the existing entry with the provided JSON body. The `name` path
parameter identifies which server to update; the body's `name` field (if
present) is ignored in favour of the path parameter.

200.description: Update an existing MCP server configuration

delete.summary: DELETE /api/mcp/servers/{name} — Remove an MCP server configuration.

post.summary: POST /api/mcp/servers/{name}/reconnect — Force a reconnect of an MCP server.

get.summary: List all proactive memories, optionally filtered by category, with pagination.

post.summary: Add memories from messages (uses extraction pipeline).

get.summary: List memories for a specific agent with pagination.

delete.summary: Delete all proactive memories for a specific agent.

post.summary: Consolidate memories for an agent: merge duplicates, cleanup stale entries.

get.summary: Find duplicate/near-duplicate memories for an agent.
Export all proactive memories for an agent as JSON.

post.summary: Import proactive memories for an agent from JSON.

get.summary: GET /api/memory/agents/:id/kv — List KV pairs for an agent.
GET /api/memory/agents/:id/kv/:key — Get a specific KV value.

delete.summary: Clear memories at a specific level (user/session/agent) for an agent.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Memory level: user, session, or agent

get.summary: Search memories scoped to a specific agent.
Get memory statistics for a specific agent.

post.summary: Clean up expired session-level memories across all agents.
post.description:
Deletes session memories older than `session_ttl_hours` (default 24).
Only session-level memories are affected — user and agent memories are persistent.

put.summary: Update a memory's content by ID.

delete.summary: Delete a specific memory by ID.

get.summary: Get the version history of a specific memory.
Search proactive memories by semantic similarity.
Get memory statistics across all agents.
Get all memories for a specific user.
GET /api/metrics — Prometheus text-format metrics.
get.description:
Returns counters and gauges for monitoring LibreFang in production:
- `librefang_agents_active` — number of active agents
- `librefang_uptime_seconds` — seconds since daemon started
- `librefang_tokens` — total tokens consumed (per agent, rolling 1h gauge)
- `librefang_tokens_input` — input tokens consumed (per agent, rolling 1h gauge)
- `librefang_tokens_output` — output tokens consumed (per agent, rolling 1h gauge)
- `librefang_tool_calls` — tool calls made (per agent, rolling 1h gauge)
- `librefang_llm_calls` — LLM API calls made (per agent, rolling 1h gauge)
- `librefang_panics_total` — supervisor panic count
- `librefang_restarts_total` — supervisor restart count
- `librefang_active_sessions` — number of active login sessions
- `librefang_cost_usd_today` — total estimated cost for today (USD)
- `librefang_http_requests_total` — HTTP request counts (with telemetry feature)
- `librefang_http_request_duration_seconds` — HTTP request latencies (with telemetry feature)

post.summary: POST /api/migrate — Run migration from another agent framework.

200.description: Run migration from another agent framework

post.summary: POST /api/migrate/scan — Scan a specific directory for OpenClaw workspace.
POST /api/models/aliases — Create a new alias mapping.
post.description: Body: `{ "alias": "my-alias", "model_id": "gpt-4o" }`

post.summary: POST /api/models/custom — Add a custom model to the catalog.
post.description:
Persists to `~/.librefang/custom_models.json` and makes the model immediately
available in the catalog.

get.summary: GET /api/network/status — OFP network status summary.

post.summary: POST /api/pairing/complete — Complete pairing with token + device info.

get.summary: GET /api/pairing/devices — List paired devices.

post.summary: POST /api/pairing/notify — Push a notification to all paired devices.
POST /api/pairing/request — Create a new pairing request (returns token + QR URI).

get.summary: GET /api/peers — List known OFP peers.
GET /api/peers/{id} — Get a single peer by node ID.
GET /api/profiles — List all tool profiles and their tool lists.
GET /api/profiles/:name — Get a single profile by name.
GET /api/providers — List all providers with auth status.
get.description:
For local providers (ollama, vllm, lmstudio), also probes reachability and
discovers available models via their health endpoints.

Probes run **concurrently** and results are **cached for 60 seconds** so the
endpoint responds instantly on repeated dashboard loads even when local
services are offline.

get.summary: GET /api/providers/github-copilot/oauth/poll/{poll_id}
get.description:
Poll the status of a GitHub device flow.
Returns `pending`, `complete`, `expired`, `denied`, or `error`.
On `complete`, saves the token to secrets.env and sets GITHUB_TOKEN.

post.summary: POST /api/providers/github-copilot/oauth/start
post.description:
Initiates a GitHub device flow for Copilot authentication.
Returns a user code and verification URI that the user visits in their browser.

get.summary: GET /api/providers/{name} — Get details for a single provider.

post.summary: POST /api/providers/{name}/default — Set a provider as the default model provider.
post.description:
Looks up the best default model for the given provider and updates both
the in-memory override and config.toml so it persists across restarts.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/providers/{name}/key — Remove an API key for a provider.

post.summary: POST /api/providers/{name}/test — Test a provider's connectivity.

put.summary: PUT /api/providers/{name}/url — Set a custom base URL for a provider.

get.summary: GET /api/queue/status — Command queue status and occupancy.

post.summary: POST /api/restore — Restore kernel state from a backup archive.
post.description:
Accepts a JSON body with `{"filename": "librefang_backup_20260315_120000.zip"}`.
The file must exist in `<home_dir>/backups/`.

**Warning**: This overwrites existing state files. The daemon should be
restarted after a restore for all changes to take effect.

POST /mcp — Handle MCP JSON-RPC requests over HTTP.
post.description:
Exposes the same MCP protocol normally served via stdio, allowing
external MCP clients to connect over HTTP instead.

200.description: Handle MCP JSON-RPC requests over HTTP

post.summary: POST /v1/chat/completions

get.summary: GET /v1/models — List available agents as OpenAI model objects.

AgentUpdateRequest.description: Request to update an agent's manifest.

AttachmentRef.description: A file attachment reference (from a prior upload).

BulkActionResult.description: Outcome of a single bulk action (delete/start/stop).

BulkAgentIdsRequest.description: Request containing a list of agent IDs for bulk operations (delete/start/stop).

BulkCreateRequest.description: Request to create multiple agents at once.

BulkCreateResult.description: Outcome of a single bulk-create item.

CallbackBody.description: POST body for the callback (programmatic clients).

state.description: State parameter for CSRF validation (signed token).

ClawHubInstallRequest.description: Request to install a skill from ClawHub.

hand.description: Install into a specific hand's workspace instead of globally.

CloneAgentRequest.description: Request body for cloning an agent.

include_skills.description: Whether to copy skills from the source agent (default: true).

include_tools.description: Whether to copy tools from the source agent (default: true).

MessageRequest.description: Request to send a message to an agent.

ephemeral.description:
If true, this is an ephemeral "side question" (`/btw`).
The message is answered using the agent's system prompt but WITHOUT
loading or saving session history — the real conversation is untouched.

group_participants.description:
Optional group participant roster (Phase 2 §C addressee guard).

Forwarded by the WhatsApp gateway for group messages so the kernel's
addressee guard (`is_addressed_to_other_participant`) can detect when
a turn is addressed to a named participant other than the agent.

`#[serde(default)]` ensures backward compatibility for callers (Telegram,
direct API) that don't populate this field.

is_group.description: Whether this message originated from a group chat (vs DM).

show_thinking.description:
Whether the response should include the model's thinking/reasoning trace.

`None` defaults to `true` when thinking content is available.

thinking.description:
Per-call deep-thinking override.

- `Some(true)`: force thinking on (even if the manifest has it off)
- `Some(false)`: force thinking off (even if the manifest has it on)
- `None`: use the manifest/global default

was_mentioned.description: Whether the bot was @mentioned in a group message.

decision_traces.description:
Decision traces from tool calls made during the agent loop.
Empty if no tools were called.

memories_saved.description:
Summaries of memories that were saved during this turn.
Empty when no new memories were extracted.

memories_used.description:
Summaries of memories that were recalled and used as context.
Empty when no relevant memories were found.

memory_conflicts.description:
Detected memory conflicts where new info contradicts existing memories.
Empty when no conflicts were detected.

thinking.description:
Combined thinking/reasoning trace from the model, when the caller
requested `show_thinking = true`.

MigrateScanRequest.description: Request to scan a directory for migration.

ParticipantRefSchema.description:
OpenAPI schema stand-in for `librefang_channels::types::ParticipantRef`.

The real type lives in `librefang-channels`, which does not depend on
utoipa. This mirror struct exists only so `#[schema(value_type = ...)]`
on `MessageRequest.group_participants` can expose the shape to the
generated OpenAPI document.

display_name.description: Human-readable name (push-name, contact name, or first part of JID).

PatchAgentConfigRequest.description: Request body for patching agent config (name, description, prompt, identity, model).

max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens for LLM response. Controls conversation window size.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature (0.0–2.0). Lower values are more deterministic.

SetAgentFileRequest.description: Request body for writing a workspace identity file.

SetModeRequest.description: Request to change an agent's operational mode.

SkillInstallRequest.description: Request to install a skill from the marketplace.

hand.description: Install into a specific hand's workspace instead of globally.

SpawnRequest.description: Request to spawn an agent from a TOML manifest string or a template name.

manifest_toml.description: Agent manifest as TOML string (optional if `template` is provided).

name.description:
Optional custom name for the agent. Overrides the name from the template
or manifest, allowing multiple agents from the same template.

signed_manifest.description:
Optional Ed25519 signed manifest envelope (JSON).
When present, the signature is verified before spawning.

template.description:
Template name from `~/.librefang/workspaces/agents/{template}/agent.toml`.
When provided and `manifest_toml` is empty, the template is loaded automatically.

UpdateIdentityRequest.description: Request body for updating agent visual identity.
